title: Requirements, ER Modeling, Functional Dependencies, and Normalization · Result-Grain Case: Normal Forms
summary: A growing incident system repeats team and member facts, mixes multiple values per cell, and produces update anomalies. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql normal forms increment in a normalized incident-system schema defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Transform repeating or partially dependent data through 1NF, 2NF, 3NF, and BCNF reasoning.
Identify functional dependencies and candidate keys from semantics rather than sample coincidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Model teams, members, tickets, and events where members may be unassigned, ticket titles repeat, and one ticket has 6 events. A defensible solution applies Normal Forms, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a normalized incident-system schema defense.

content.code:
SELECT ticket_id, status, opened_at
FROM tickets
WHERE status <> 'resolved'
ORDER BY opened_at, ticket_id;
-- Record SQLite behavior, then verify syntax and semantics against PostgreSQL 18.4.

);
